title: Alert: Brent crude spikes to $126/barrel on US-Iran military signalling
summary: Dataminr real-time alerting flagged a sudden Brent crude oil price spike to $126 per barrel on April 30, the highest level in over four years, triggered by reports of fresh US military options against Iran ahead of CENTCOM's briefing to President Trump.
contents:
# Alert: Brent crude spikes to $126/barrel on US-Iran military signalling

## Detection Event
Dataminr's real-time event detection platform issued a market-impact alert on April 30 flagging a sudden Brent crude oil price spike to $126 per barrel, the highest level recorded in more than four years. The platform correlated the price move with reporting that CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper would brief President Trump the same day on additional military options against Iran, including a potential special operations forces raid against the Iranian highly enriched uranium stockpile.

## Verification Path
Dataminr's algorithmic correlation flagged simultaneous social-media chatter, futures-market price action, and news-wire ticker activity, generating a high-confidence alert within minutes of the price move. The platform also tracked secondary impacts: airline futures, mid-stream energy equity moves, and shipping-insurance rate adjustments.

## Operational Significance
The alert exemplifies Dataminr's pattern of providing minutes-to-hours lead time over traditional financial-media reporting on event-driven market moves. Subscribers received the alert before mainstream broadcast coverage. Brent eased slightly later in the day as the White House withheld immediate comment on Cooper's briefing options.
